REMOVED FROM COURT TRIAL CALENDAR ON OCT-02-2017 IN DEPT. 220, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE EVIDENCE SHOWED THAT THE BOARD, AND DEFENDANT LIN ACTED IN GOOD FAITH, UPON REASONABLE INQUIRY, AND WITHIN ITS AUTHORITY IN ADOPTING THE BUSINESS HOURS RULES. THE EVIDENCE DOES NOT PREPONDERATE TO SHOW ANY DAMAGES OCCASIONED TO PLAINTIFF BY VIRTUE OF THE RULE ADOPTION. THE BOARD PROPERLY, AND BEFORE THE INITIATION OF LITIGATION, AND UNDER NEW MANAGEMENT, SOUGHT PROFESSIONAL ASSISTANCE IN DETERMINING PROPER UTILITY CHARGES. THE AUDIT OF EXPENSES WAS PROPERLY UNDERTAKEN, AND THAT AUDIT SHOWED PLAINTIFF OWES DEFENDANT ASSOCIATION AS SHOWN IN ROBERT ADACHI'S TESTIMONY AND EXHIBITS 210-216. ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFF SHALL TAKE NOTHING BY ITS COMPLAINT BY WAY OF DAMAGES OR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. DECLARATORY RELIEF IS DETERMINED AS SET FORTH ABOVE. COSTS AND FEES SHALL BE DETERMINED IN ACCORDANCE WITH LAW. IF PLAINTIFF TIMELY REQUESTS A WRITTEN STATEMENT OF DECISION, COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS SHALL PREPARE AND CIRCULATE SUCH STATEMENT AS REQUIRED BY STATUTE AND RULE. FORMAL JUDGMENT SHALL BE PREPARED BY COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS, SUBMITTED TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS CONFORMING TO THIS DETERMINATION, AND SUBMITTED TO THE COURT FOR FILING AND SERVICE. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-16-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
John A. Kithas, of Law Offices of John A. Kithas, Tel. 415.788.8100, for plaintiffs;
Jeff Cluett, of Freeman Mathis & Gary LLP, Tel. 415.394.9500, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants Miriwa Center Condominium Owners Association and Kao Lin's motion for summary judgment is denied, and defendants Kinry C. Louie, and Kinry & Associates, LLC's motion for summary judgment is granted. While the first amendment to the complaint moots the portion of the motion based on lack of standing by former plaintiff Donald Cheng, the Kinry defendants are entitled to summary judgment in their favor because all three claims alleged against them fail because they are not parties to the CC&Rs and do not own any units or other property in the building. Because the Kinry defendants are not parties to the CC&Rs and do not own any units in the building, there is no basis to seek declaratory, injunctive or damages relief against them based on the CC&Rs. Plaintiff's request to deny the motion per CCP 437c(h) lacks merit since plaintiff has not shown that any facts may exist which would enable it to defeat the Kinry defendants' motion. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
